text.title: Faion CLI as agent skill — core rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for wiring `faion` into a custom agent.

rule.statement: Per-turn budget: ≤3 tool calls to `faion_*` AND ≤2000 tokens of fetched content. The dispatcher enforces; the agent must not retry past the cap.
rule.rationale: Unbounded fan-out blows the budget and pulls competing methodologies into context, where the agent picks the wrong one.

rule.statement: Tool args are typed: `faion_search(query:string, tier_hint:string|null, max_results:int=5)`, `faion_get_content(slug:string, file:string="AGENTS.md")`. Free-form arg objects are rejected at the schema layer.
rule.rationale: Typed args make the call auditable and let the dispatcher reject malformed agent output before it hits the CLI subprocess.

rule.statement: On `403 tier_required`, the dispatcher returns the embedded preview (≤200 chars) + the tier upgrade hint; the agent MUST surface this to the user instead of retrying the same slug.
rule.rationale: Retries on tier-gated content waste budget and never succeed. The preview is the actionable path.

rule.statement: `faion_get_content` calls are idempotent — the same slug + file pair MUST return identical bytes for a given CLI version. The dispatcher caches by (slug,file,cli_version).
rule.rationale: Without caching the agent re-fetches the same AGENTS.md every turn, multiplying cost and token budget.

rule.statement: No customer PII flows into the `query` arg of `faion_search`. The dispatcher strips emails, phone numbers, and credit-card-shaped tokens before subprocess invocation.
rule.rationale: `faion search` may forward queries to the user's own LLM provider for rewrites — PII in the query is a data-handling incident.

rule.statement: Every tool call is logged with: (timestamp, tool_name, args_redacted, latency_ms, status, cached_bool). Logs feed the per-turn budget and post-hoc audits.
rule.rationale: Without observability the budget rule (r1) is unenforceable and incident reproduction is impossible.

rule.statement: All rules satisfied: tool-defs match the JSON Schema, system prompt cites budget + 403 path, dispatcher implements cache + PII strip + logging.
No tool-use loop, no CLI access, or anonymous tier — the integration is impossible until those preconditions are fixed.
